etymology: Felix = happy/fortunate; Buckman = man of the buck/deer
wordplay_note: Happy man who is deeply unhappy; police general who weeps
card_description: Felix Buckman (meaning "Felix = happy/fortunate; Buckman = man of the buck/deer") is a character in Philip K. Dick's *Flow My Tears the Policeman Said*. Police general of the totalitarian regime who undergoes an epiphany of compassion. Name note: Happy man who is deeply unhappy; police general who weeps. The tears of the policeman — even an authoritarian functionary can experience agape. Appears in 10 Exegesis passages. First appears in *Flow My Tears the Policeman Said*. Context: "Dick explores the "Lived Myth" (Joseph Campbell) and "Ma'at" (Right Order) as keys to his 1974 experience."
first_work: Flow My Tears the Policeman Said
work_list: Flow My Tears the Policeman Said
